reviews.review_description: If everything was closer to you i would give it 5 stars. I don't have G. Antetokoumpo's arms, i can't reach anything without leaving my chair. Every time i wanted to play i remembered this and said fak it... i don't want to break my back.

I purchased this because in the reviews it stated that it was multiplayer game. I purchased it along with my friend and we tried to find the multiplayer settings but after reading more information on the game it stated that it was a single user game. Now there is no option for a refund.

developer_response.body:
Hi. In no place on our store page do we claim this is a multiplayer game. In fact, the store page specifically says it's Single User.

For a refund, please contact Meta: https://www.meta.com/help/quest/828412189021014/

And giving our game a 1-star review because you can't read is frustrating and disappointing. Please be a better human being. Thanks!

reviews.review_description:
I mainly write this to say that there are no problems running this on Quest 3 for me, since I read that in a review.

Also, this game is very hard, as there are no real explanations. But the world, machines and "story" (if you want to call it that) around you are so fascinating that I alwas wanted to keep going even if I had no idea what to do next. And this game looks pretty good, too.
Still haven't finished it completely, but I am sure I will!
It's not an escape room. There are no hints or tutorials. It is hard to figure what you are supposed to be doing or going.
This is like turning up to work hungover and being sent to do something you have no idea how to do. I love it

Easily one of my favorite VR games and I've played many. The game doesn't hold your hand and you really need to experiment with the machine to figure it out. 
The graphics are great, easily on par with some PCVR games, and I loved the art style. 
The fact that you can reach almost everything without having to walk around within your play space made it probably the most immersive game in played in VR.
I really hope the devs make another game similar to this, maybe even a continuation of this one.

Does not work on the quest 3 device.  Game launches and you are stuck on the menu to choose language. Button and dial control does not respond at all. Did a system reboot and no change in the game.  Not compatible with quest 3 at this time.

I was on the fence about  getting this game for such a long time due to the reviews that said it was too hard so never bought till I recently saw it in sale and decided to try it. Man am I glad I did. Graphics and mood/environment is pretty cool. Reminded me a bit of Myst in terms of the challenge of figuring out what to do. Really enjoyed it.

First time in my Quest 2 ownership that a game just won’t work. Once I finally got it to download and tried to Start the game, it froze. Nothing would ever go past the first chamber within the language menu. I uninstalled it and tried to start over after several said to do that. Won’t install. I spent 3 days and ran through the battery and it disappeared and went to the previously installed section. After several months of trying to fix this i would have to say i am not happy. Sent emails  messages so. Ow this review.

If you like puzzle games and if you also liked Iron Lung, this game is for you, my playthrough took about 2 hours, but i’d say it might take 1-1.5 hours for a good puzzler. I wouldn’t recommend buying it on it’s own full price, so get it on sale or in a bundle, I got it in the bundle alongside Myst, for a game I thought “Eh, it looks decent and I might as well get it since I want Myst” but it decently surprised me, no tutorials or tips or hints, this game gives you no hand holding and leaves you to figure everything out. It only gives you a basic understanding of what to do through a tablet. There are 4 rooms in total, and fun gameplay, with roguelike mechanics, if you die, your Nauticrawl is where it died and you have to grab the stuff. Short game, would recommend if you see it in a bundle

Warning: this review contains spoilers.
Basically, 80% of the game is you figuring out the mechanics of each control station; like the crawler module. Once you’ve figured them out, and a couple of new tricks, you’re good to go. The story feels underdeveloped for me, though, and more content is needed, but since the main game IS the mechanics, I won’t complain. Also, what is that ending? Like if you’re about to shoot through outer space, why are the windows open? Shouldn’t they be closed? And why does the outside still look like you’re still here? Then there’s the ‘you shouldn’t be here’ message at the end of your start through space; ending up with you back at the elevator. I’m theorizing that either this MIGHT be a daydream as you hear breathing when you transition back to the elevator, or simply the capitol caught you, before you warped out of this planet, and decided to play along with your ‘escape’. The crawler module detaching from your cabin ship is a bit suspicious as if the capitol is preparing you for transport back to the one place you wanted to escape from, or I’m overthinking this. I also know that this was a ramble, but I don’t really care.
,

You are put in the cockpit of a massive futuristic vehicle with only a tablet for direction that is as clear as Ikea instructions. At one point I actually completed the task of opening the gate but I didn't know it because I wasn't looking at the map to see what actually opened (I thought the gate was the thing I was interacting with on radar to open but I just ended up ramming myself into the wall). I watched the video walk thru and realized that if I knew how a car works it may have been easier, you spend half the game balancing fuel, battery power, engine power and engine heat just to move.  The other half you are watching a radar screen to navigate like you are in a submarine. In the end, tasks that require you to flip switches, turn dials and push buttons in a very particular order is not as fun for me as it may be for someone else. I gave it 3 stars because you can tell the developers did put a lot of thought into it and it is beautifully rendered, it's just not what I consider a fun puzzle.

I love this game with ALL of my loins. I knew nothing going in and I highly recommend doing the same if you can. From the very start I thought I was adjusting my height and then realized I was already playing the game. When you don’t know what the limitations/borders are of a game, they’re temporarily endless and this game really delivers that feel. Thanks for a superb experience!

When I first played this game, I was thinking "HOW ARE THERE THAT MANY LEVERS?" and so I experimented. If I press this is turns on. If I pull this I move forward. If I flip this I see that. The magic of this game I learning what everything does and the developers have done an amazing job with it. The only thing I wish is that there was more.

It really isn't as hard as people make it out to be, plus people say you get no help, but I came across multiple log entries while playing the game that give hints or straight up fully explain some functionalities. Granted, this isn't a game for everyone, but for me I found it relatively easy to figure out. Amazing graphics and amazing concept 10/10

It wouldn't take much, just a little help and this could be a top title. Unfortunately, I didn't have a clue. Myst was easy, Fisherman's Tale was fun, Red Matter was terrific... this one? Impenetrable. 

Odd choice, make a game so difficult to play people just give up and buy something else, which I did.

This game is really one of a kind. The people who complain that it's too difficult and rate poorly because of that don't deserve to play it.
The immersion is outstanding: reading gauges, interacting with terminals, toggling switches and pulling levers has never been so cool! It's really interesting to tinker with everything and learn how it works.

Sadly, the game is reaaaally short. I finished it in less than two hours. Two fantastic hours for sure, but still. It really wanted to spend more time in that machine.

I love puzzle games. From I Expect You to Die to The Room to Red Mars to Moss. Love 'em. Yet I've spent over six hours in the first room for this one. Even looked at a walkthrough but because of various constraints in the game - and its opaque gameplay - I can't get out of the first room . It would take a short essay to describe all the problems and frustrations I've had. 

As I've said, very happy with escape room games. But this is - for me - just a frustrating horrible mess. It's possible I'm being obtuse but all the other puzzle  games I've played on VR were just fine. I'm trying one more time and if I can't get out of room 1 I'm deleting permanently. 

In a word: aaaaarghhhh!!!!

(This won't mean anything unless you've played the game). Just went in again. I can nethook up to the crystal tower, but my coat of arms is wrong. Cloaking device won't help. I seem to remember I picked up something to do with faking your coat of arms but this was before I died in the game. I went back to the worker but couldn't find the coat of arms thing. I've been here about 15 times. I've never found a game so completely frustrating. I've deleted permanently from device. And I'm taking a star off.

reviews.review_title: Someone is going to love this game…
reviews.review_description:
Someone is going to love this game. It isn’t me.

I thought this was going to be more like I Expect You to Die. While it shares some small part of that gameplay it has its own approach to puzzle solving.

I was an escapee using a mech to navigate a maze to freedom. The entire game takes place within the cockpit and the only contact with the outside world is a radar screen and a computer monitor providing limited information.

There are two components to the game: figuring out how to operate the mech and navigating the maze while managing some challenges along the way.

The graphics are highly detailed, but after all I only saw the cockpit. The story is limited and fed slowly but there is some mystery that kept my interest. The controls are sequential at first and this makes them easy to figure out. It is the gameplay that ruined it for me.

The gameplay is essentially repeating the same procedures over and over dozens of times until you figure out the right path through the maze and its obstacles. You have to start over again and again from the beginning until you do so. For this reason the game quickly became tedious and boring to me. It needed to interject something to break up that monotony- humor, a voice, some other interaction, more information on the computer, etc.

 Very early into the first mission I realized, “Oh no, this game isn’t for me.”

If you like procedural grinding this will be the game for you.

Overall it’s somewhat exhausting to play. It’s confusing to work out, and once you do, it’s still not clear what you should be trying to do or why you should care.

Having all info on the screens is somewhat exhausting. 

Having audio cues when you make progress or do something right (as I Expect You To Die does) would go along way. And having some info given by voice rather than tedious displays would be nice.

I’m guaranteeing you will have no idea what you are doing when you first start, and honestly I was all in! If you were a fan of the Room VR, you will definitely like this game. Almost nothing is explained to you, and you need to figure out how to do everything on your own. I’m only a couple of hours in and am already engrossed! It is refreshing to play a game that doesn’t try and pander to the player, instead challenging them to figure things out on their own.

reviews.review_title: Looks great and runs great…slow burn.
reviews.review_description:
****This game is essentially 
“I expect you to die” 
with out a sense of humor. 

***Make sure your play space is big enough.. for real. 

I just started in to it this evening.. so far I have basically already become a mech-pilot…sooo yeah..skillllss.. 

I’m not at all familiar with this universe.. so it’s all new to me. Looks great on Q2 runs great even at high resolution. The button pushing is a little dumpy I have to mess with it for the finger to point.. it would be great to have gloves or hands and not just ghost hands.. but whatever that’s small stuff. Otherwise I am intrigued.. I have no idea what I’m doing.. as I imagine most people will feel.. a lot of folks will drop this game pretty quickly if they are expecting a faster paced game. Look.. it’s a puzzle game.. and when you mess up… you start alll over lol.. good times. I love the mechanics though. Gets the brain going.

My headset has died twice on me playing this. Should be a sign it’s pretty dang good. Its more than just escape room, it is a game. 

It has a learning curve, and it is rewarding when you can move around after really learning how it works. Very immersive when you just flipping switches to turn things back on or starting up the power, simple stuff to us, but it is what makes you forget about reality. 

I do not know where I am towards to end, but I have drained my headset 2 times so it has some depth to it. Graphics great, puzzles are not too difficult but like I said it is a game as well. It is a little hard to explain but do not expect normal “puzzles”, it is more just trying to figure out what to do, and when you do, playing the game. Def one of a kind game atm! Bravo

Ps: maybe I didn’t see it right, but was the opening menu a puzzle? If so that was amazing idea. Or I could have just been playing the game 🤣 I don’t know at this point.
So I’ve just done my first run on this game gotta say it’s fun but half the time it feels like you don’t know what you are doing though the variety is fun
Discovery fun

*possible spoilers* when I first started this game it seemed kinda boring because it seemed like there was only one area and not very many places to go, but once you unlock some of the other stations it becomes so much more fun and there are much more encounters including huge sounds that you need to zap and electric gates. for a story game this game is definitely replayable just for how much you interact with things and for encounters and trading.

reviews.review_title: Take the time to enjoy it
reviews.review_description: This game is not for someone who wants to hop right in and be good. Very little instruction on how to get going, but a great puzzle and an even bigger feel of accomplishment when you start to get rolling. Graphics are amazing, sound and haptic feedback in the controller are on par with any game out there. Pick it up and give it a try!

I would actually give this 4.5 stars if I could.  The only reason I would not give it a full 5 stars is because of the way your ghost hands work.  To press a button, you have to get the hands fairly close to the button that you want to press, and then hit the button.  There is no way to point your finger beforehand and then move your hand forward to press a button, like most games allow you to do.  I suppose this might be because of the type and number of different controls you have to use, but it seems like it could be worked out.  You'll definitely want to have the recommended play space size to truly enjoy this game too.  Also, I've played it a few hours now and have yet to die, so I think how often you die must depend a bit on how you play games like this.

I would compare this game more to MYST, but you're confined to a cockpit (or two or three ;).  If you're careful and look around a bit before going nuts pressing buttons, levers, wheels, etc., you might find something that could be of help to you when you first start playing.

The graphics do look pretty good, and it plays smoothly on my Quest 2.  The gameplay/story has been quite fun and creative so far, and I've found it enjoyable (so much so, that in my first run on a fairly freshly charged Quest 2, I was getting the super-low battery warning before I knew it, so I had to quit!)  Also, although not mentioned anywhere that I've seen, this appears to be a cross-buy with the Rift.  So I just grabbed that version for free.

My family and I love escape room games, and this one scratched our holiday itch. We cast to the TV and take turns in the headset as we get stumped. This game was particularly immersive. I felt like I was actually inside a mech trying to figure out how to make it run and avoid enemies. I don’t mind the lack of hints and answers when this game brings our family together to work as a team to solve the puzzles. The eeriness is similar to that of exploring the worlds of Myst, but this game involves enemies you have to avoid or destroy. Overall, an incredible experience if you appreciate challenging puzzles and have patience and persistence to stick with it!

reviews.review_title: Great game, too short for price.
reviews.review_description: Figuring out the mech mechanics was an absolute blast, and honestly not as complicated as some in other reviews make it out to be. It also gives you a little tablet to give a basic rundown of the ship mechanics, which some could consider a bit of a bummer. Otherwise, the gameplay was great from start to finish, and it was a blast to play, with my main complaint being that this game is way too short. I had started and finished the game in what feels to be about 3 hours, which is way too short for a 20$ dollar game in my opinion. I would wait till it's on sale for at most 15$.

reviews.review_title: One of the best experiences in vr
reviews.review_description: This is one of the best experiences I have had in vr, usually I don't write reviews but I really believe this game deserves all the 5 stars it can get. my only complaint with the game is that I can't go back in time to experience the beginning again, a massive part of the game is figuring out what does what as theres close to no tutorial and all the info you get about the mech is through clever means be it via the computer, coms or whatever way the game fits in info, everything about the game feels so unique and kinda eerie. overall this is a game you need to experience on your own and 1000000% recommend to anyone especially if you are into escape rooms/puzzle games

reviews.review_title: I like it needs more stuff
reviews.review_description: Very fun a little hard and creepy please add level select and access to all levels and multiplayer mode really cool graphics perfect performance  I played for a month non stop🤔❤️😸 hard to control

reviews.review_title: I love the idea of it but need more help
reviews.review_description:
I was intrigued by the theme of this one. And I love the look of it and the feel of driving this giant machine. But after I finally made it through the first level I just kept failing. I think for someone like me who is just a very casual gamer, a game like this needs more hints and more ways to get help in the game. I remember for one part of the first level I actually had to get off the VR and look up a YouTube video in order to figure out what it was that I was missing. I shouldn't have to do that. There should be in-game hints or suggestions and there were but there weren't enough. 

And because there are no windows and you have to drive this thing on radar alone, it makes it very frustrating. I can't figure out for the life of me what I need to do to stop running low on power and fuel. I've tried so many things and I'm at a loss. Plus in level 2 I'm not even sure what I'm looking for and the computers add to the confusion. It's an interesting game, but for me it's too much like work.

I could see a lot of people hating on this one for the lack of instructions but I personally love the idea of having to figure everything out on your own. It feels pretty good to go from “what the heck does this lever do” to pro-mech operator all on your own. Only completed a few runs so far but excited to keep going back to explore! Graphics look great on Q2 and the controls work exactly like they should. The oculus store is overrun with subpar lack luster games so it’s nice to pick up one I don’t feel like returning immediately after trying it out.

I really like the escape room games available on Quest VR; 'I Expect You to Die', 'The Room VR', 'Down the Rabbit Hole', 'Shadow Point', etc... 
A Rogue Escape is different and I love it - no voice over, clues, prompts or rewards. You are just dropped into a situation that you have to solve and the gameplay is part of the puzzle.
I am really enjoying this game.
But there is no moving or teleporting -  make sure you have the 6ft x 6ft game space - you need to operate within the environment.

reviews.review_title: If you are thinking of buying
reviews.review_description: If your thinking of buying, and don’t have I expect you to die, buy that instead

If RoomVR married an 80’s text based DND maze crawler, this game would be their child. The VR is fantastic, and the visual puzzles are fun, but be aware that  a huge part of the game is reading text descriptions and and exploring large mazes on a tiny radar screen.

Think of if as a fantastic simulation of a computer complex, where the object of the game is to play a text adventure on simulated PC. Its a little unfair, but that is what it felt like. I could even have forgiven the maze crawl aspect, but as soon as you get out of that part, you get maybe 15 minutes of new stuff and then dumped back into a new maze.

At first, I thought the game was going to be too short, and later I just wanted it to be over. I’m guessing there are 4 mazes and I’m still stuck in the 2nd. There doesn’t seem to be any VR left, just text adventure maze crawler from here on.

4 stars instead of 3 because I haven’t completed it. It might finish strong.

UPDATE: Fun fact; if you don’t quit the game by holding down X, it doesn’t save your progress. So I got to play it again from the start, and this time finished in one sitting (maybe 1 or 2 hours?). On both play throughs, it only required 2 mazes (plus a tiny third). I don’t know if I chose luckily or if it just always puts what you need in the second maze. I wouldn’t say it finished strong, but it did have more VR puzzles at the end. I’ll leave it at 4 stars, but its a weak 4th star.

This game is wonderful! I've played it for hours and hours and haven't gotten bored with it yet. I can see some people not enjoying the game due to the difficulty since there is almost nothing to go off of. The graphics look great on quest and the game has been running perfectly! I haven't found in noticeable glitches with anything yet. It's a one of a kind game that is worth every dollar for the experience!!! A 10 of 10 must have

There are no instructions right from the beginning and you don’t even really know what you’re supposed to be doing. You start figuring things out and putting pieces together and finally you know exactly what you’re doing how to do it. Very fun and unique game, right when I mastered everything I was right at the end of the game! Wish it was longer :)

reviews.review_title: A Mixture of Visual and Imagination
reviews.review_description: This unique game takes a minute to learn the ins and outs of manipulating the nauticrawl, which is a big part of this lovely adventure. I'm looking forward to the second installment!
